title: Docker
create.fromExamples: Create from examples. Click {{use}} to create a new container template.
create.hubExplorerDesc: Search image names and pick a tag before pulling.
create.customDesc: Define runtime options line-by-line, then create once.
container.noStopped: No stopped containers from current Docker daemon/context.
container.remove.title: Remove Container
ports.title: Containers and Docker Ports column
cleanup.title: Guided System Cleanup
cleanup.pruneContainersDesc: Removes all containers that are not currently running.
cleanup.pruneImagesDesc: Removes images that are not referenced by any containers.
cleanup.pruneVolumesDesc: Removes all local volumes not used by at least one container.
cleanup.pruneNetworksDesc: Removes all networks not used by at least one container.
wizard.title: Docker Setup Wizard
wizard.noFlatpak: Automated install is not available in Flatpak
wizard.available: Automated install available on this session
wizard.sudoPlaceholder: Sudo password (optional, leave blank for Polkit)
wizard.step.refreshDashboard: Refresh the dashboard to detect the daemon.
wizard.step.verify: Run docker version in your host terminal to verify.
wizard.step.permissions: If access is denied, log out and back in to refresh group permissions.
inspect.portBindings: Port bindings. Apply recreates the container.
inspect.networkMode: Network mode. Apply recreates the container.
inspect.envVars: Environment variables. Apply recreates the container.
ports.listDesc: Lists every container. The Host publish column is "yes" only when docker ps shows a binding like 0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp (that is what Remap can clone and change).
ports.remapDesc: Clones the container with a new host port binding, then stops/removes the original when possible. Container must be running or stoppable as Docker allows.
cleanup.freeUpDesc: Free up disk space by removing unused Docker resources. Select what to prune:
cleanup.safetyNoteDesc: Cleanup only removes resources Docker reports as unused. Running containers are not removed.
page.label: DOCKER.SURFACE
page.title: Docker
page.desc: Full Docker surface from one click UI: containers, images, volumes, networks, and cleanup.
stats.empty: Stats only available for running containers.
volume.descAnonymous: Unused Anonymous Volume. Leftover data from a deleted container. Safe to remove if unneeded.
volume.descAnonymousUsed: Anonymous Volume. Automatically created by a running container to store internal data.
volume.descNamed: Named Volume. Specifically created to persist important database or application data safely.
network.descBridge: Default network. Connects containers together and provides internet access.
network.descHost: Removes network isolation. Containers share the host's exact IP and ports.
network.descNone: Completely disables networking. Container has no internet or local access.
network.descCompose: Custom bridge network (usually created by Docker Compose) to isolate an app.
